run_policy.protocol: Present each prompt as a Skill-selection task using only installed Skill names and descriptions. Open every selected SKILL.md so loading is observable, report selected names, and do not solve the underlying task.

cases.prompt: Before we author a new Skill for recurring release handoffs, decide whether a Skill is actually the right intervention. Use our completed examples, define the responsibility and trigger boundary, and design the evaluation and implementation handoff.
Two Skills both activate for API incident triage. Design whether to merge them or keep separate responsibilities, including metadata, coexistence risks, and evaluation cases.
This Skill now handles planning, destructive execution, and post-change validation. Design a coherent split or rescope before anyone edits it.
Turn this proven data-export workflow and its recurring corrections into an implementation-ready Skill design. Decide which knowledge belongs in instructions, references, scripts, and evals.

Substantially redesign our migration Skill so fragile steps use a verified script while contextual decisions remain flexible. Define preserved safety properties and the candidate-vs-baseline evaluation.
Audit why this existing SKILL.md behaves inconsistently across clients. Diagnose loading, precedence, trigger conflicts, and evidence before recommending changes.
Fix two typos and simplify one sentence in this SKILL.md without changing its behavior or trigger.
The Skill design is approved. Edit the listed files, add the agreed script, run the specified tests, and report the diff.
Design the database and API changes for adding organization-level billing to this application, including risks and verification.
The Skill responsibility is already settled. Run repeated trigger experiments and tune only its description based on false-positive and false-negative results.
Normalize this SKILL.md to CommonMark spacing and list syntax without changing any wording or behavior.
